answer: Treat the public pack as candidate methods, not authority. Do a read-only source review before any install or activation, inspect executable and persistent surfaces, sample trigger clarity, verification, negative cases, and scope boundaries, then extract mechanisms or eval ideas before any active behavior change.

Classify payment messages and account statements as sensitive financial data. Require review before install, ingest, MCP registration, WebSocket streaming, persistent storage, graph query, export, or agent-client connection. Review package provenance, lifecycle scripts, data classification, identifiers, storage, retention, endpoint binding, logging, schemas, redaction, audit, teardown, and legal or policy boundaries.

Treat generated graph artifacts and summaries as derived memory about private source material. Review installer scripts, lifecycle scripts, symlink or plugin targets, runtime load paths, subagent prompts, scan roots, ignore files, artifact paths, source and secret exposure, commit policy, freshness, local server binding, auto-update hooks, cost, rollback, and deletion path. Require explicit scope before scanning private repositories, enabling hooks, launching dashboards, committing graphs, or sharing artifacts.
